mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below the 4.0% threshold, it signals a tight labor market that historically precedes wage-driven consumption growth, driving positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops below 4.0% from a prior reading above 4.2%
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following UNRATE crossings below 4.0% do not exceed the median return of all trading days by at least 0.5% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and nonfarm payrolls growth confirms a synchronized expansion in residential investment and consumer spending, providing a robust demand backdrop that sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between HOUST and PAYEMS growth rates shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During early-cycle expansions following a recession, industrial production growth acts as a leading indicator of corporate earnings recovery, producing positive 5-day forward returns as market participants price in the cyclical upturn.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes fail to predict 5-day forward returns with a directional accuracy above 55% during the first 12 months following a NBER recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index breaches the 250.0 level, it indicates entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0 for two consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL sustains values above 250.0, contradicting the multiple compression premise.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail and food services sales and real GDP confirms broad-based economic momentum, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to produce a positive predictive information coefficient (IC) greater than 0.05 for 20-day forward returns across three consecutive decades.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in total nonfarm payrolls consistently signals labor market resilience, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day forward window.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises average below zero or show no statistical difference from baseline returns over a 10-year walk-forward validation period.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle overheating regimes characterized by peak housing activity and elevated interest rates, a decline in housing starts serves as an early warning of demand contraction, triggering negative 5-day forward returns as equity markets price in the impending slowdown.
failure_condition: HOUST declines during peak housing periods do not precede negative 5-day forward returns in at least 50% of observed instances, or the signal's hit rate drops below random chance over a 3-year test window.
